name: Purdue
description_short: Purdue Federal Credit Union is an Indiana-based credit union offering checking, savings, loans, and investment services to members, with special programs for Purdue University and Ivy Tech students.
description_long: Purdue Federal Credit Union (PFCU) is a member-owned financial cooperative headquartered in Indiana that has built its identity around serving the Purdue University and broader educational community. The credit union operates multiple physical branches across Indiana, including locations in Lafayette, West Lafayette, Crown Point, and La Porte, while also offering nationwide branch and ATM access to members. Founded with deep ties to Purdue University—positioning itself as the "Official Credit Union for Purdue Fans"—the institution has expanded to serve Ivy Tech students, high school students, and general consumers seeking credit union membership. PFCU offers a comprehensive suite of financial products spanning deposit accounts (checking, savings, money market, share certificates, IRAs, HSAs), lending products (auto loans, home loans, HELOCs, personal loans, student loans, business loans including SBA 504s), credit cards (Visa with rewards options), and digital banking services. The credit union advertises competitive rates including auto loans as low as 5.44% APR, HELOCs at 6.75% APR, savings accounts yielding up to 3.91% APY, and promotional 0% introductory APR on Visa Signature cards. They also offer specialized business services including business checking, lines of credit, equipment financing, and treasury management. What distinguishes PFCU is its intentional focus on student financial empowerment and life-stage services. The credit union provides targeted student checking and debit card products for Purdue, Ivy Tech, and high school students, plus educational resources like "Boiler Financial Track" and "The Financial Vine" programs. Their "My Member Perks" program and cash rewards offerings add value beyond traditional banking. The institution emphasizes community involvement and member testimonials, positioning itself as a trusted financial partner rather than a transactional bank. However, membership appears restricted to specific groups or those with qualifying affiliations (Purdue/Ivy Tech students, certain employee groups, or those meeting other membership criteria not fully detailed on the site). As a credit union rather than a traditional bank, PFCU may have more limited branch/ATM access than national banks, though they advertise nationwide availability. The website does not clearly specify all membership eligibility requirements, which could create barriers for some prospective customers. Rates advertised are competitive but not necessarily market-leading, and like most financial institutions, they charge various fees for services.
services: Savings accounts and high-yield money market accounts
Share certificates and IRA money market accounts
Auto loans and auto refinancing with cash back
Home loans and home equity lines of credit (HELOCs)
Visa credit cards (traditional rewards and signature with introductory 0% APR)
Business checking, savings, and money market accounts
SBA 504 loans and business lines of credit
pros: Specialized student financial programs for Purdue University and Ivy Tech students with dedicated checking/debit cards and educational resources
Competitive loan rates including auto loans as low as 5.44% APR and HELOCs at 6.75% APR with 1.5% cash back up to $1,500 on auto refinances
High-yield savings options with money market accounts and special share certificates offering up to 3.91% APY
Comprehensive product suite including personal loans, home equity lines of credit, business loans (SBA 504), and multiple credit card options
Member benefits program (My Member Perks) and cash rewards offerings that add value beyond standard banking
Physical branch locations in Indiana plus nationwide branch and ATM access for convenience
Educational resources including financial empowerment programs, calculators, fraud resources, and the Money Moves blog
cons: Membership eligibility appears restricted to specific groups (Purdue/Ivy Tech affiliates, select employee groups), limiting accessibility for general consumers
Website does not clearly outline all membership requirements upfront, creating potential friction in the signup process
As a credit union, likely fewer physical locations than national banks despite nationwide ATM access claims
Personal loan APR starts at 11.40%, which is notably higher than some online personal loan competitors
Limited transparency on fees schedule and service charges compared to major financial institutions
best_for: Purdue University students and alumni seeking specialized student banking and educational financial services
Ivy Tech students needing accessible credit union banking with student-focused products and rates
Indiana residents with ties to Purdue or select employee groups looking for member-owned banking with community focus
Consumers seeking competitive auto loans and home equity financing with cash back incentives
diagnosis: Purdue Federal Credit Union is best for Purdue University and Ivy Tech students, alumni, and Indiana residents with qualifying affiliations who value student-focused financial education and competitive credit union rates. The main caveat is that membership eligibility appears restricted to specific groups, and prospective members should verify their qualification before applying, as the website does not provide complete transparency on all eligibility requirements.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Indiana-based credit union offering checking, savings, loans, and investment services for Purdue and Ivy Tech students.
